headline: Citi Cuts Palantir Price Target to $210 Amid AI Software Multiple Compression
summary: Citi lowered its price target on PLTR to $210 from $260 while maintaining Buy rating, citing recent multiple compression across the AI software sector. PLTR stock fell to ~$137 range.

headline: OpenAI Revenue Misses Internal Targets; Anthropic Gains Enterprise Share
summary: OpenAI fell short of several monthly revenue targets in 2026 as Anthropic gained ground with enterprise and developer clients. ChatGPT did not reach 1B weekly active users by end of 2025. Oracle and CoreWeave stocks declined on the news.

headline: Microsoft and OpenAI End Exclusivity Agreement — AWS and Google Cloud Can Now Sell OpenAI Models
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI restructured their alliance, ending exclusive distribution rights that previously allowed only Azure to resell OpenAI models. OpenAI is now expanding to AWS, opening new revenue channels but diluting Azure competitive advantage.

headline: AI Talent War Escalates: OpenAI and Anthropic Poach Senior Executives from Salesforce, Snowflake, Datadog
summary: Top executives from enterprise software companies are jumping to AI model developers, lured by large compensation packages. Salesforce stock is off 32% YTD partly due to talent drain concerns.

headline: Hyperscaler Capex on Track to Exceed $650B in 2026 — 36% YoY Increase
summary: Combined capex for Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Oracle is projected to exceed $650B in 2026, with ~75% allocated to AI infrastructure (~$450B). Amazon leads with ~$200B guided capex, Google at ~$175B, Microsoft ~$120B+, Meta $115-135B.

headline: CoreWeave and Meta Expand AI Infrastructure Deal to $21B Through 2032
summary: CoreWeave announced expanded long-term agreement with Meta Platforms to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion. Wall Street analysts lifted price forecasts following the announcement.

headline: Oracle and CoreWeave Drop on OpenAI Growth Concerns
summary: Oracle fell ~4% to $166 and CoreWeave dropped ~6% to $106 after reports flagged OpenAI revenue target misses, raising questions about sustainability of AI infrastructure demand.

headline: IEA: Datacenter Electricity Use Surged 17% in 2025, AI-Focused Centers Growing Even Faster
summary: Global datacenter electricity consumption set to double by 2030, with AI-focused datacenters power use poised to triple. SMR conditional offtake pipeline grew from 25 GW (end 2024) to 45 GW currently.

headline: Constellation Energy Declares Dividend, Three Mile Island Restart Progresses with Microsoft
summary: CEG declared $0.4265/share dividend and continues strategic partnership with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island nuclear plant. Stock trading at ~$306 with $111B market cap.

headline: Vistra Expected to Report 206% EPS Growth in Q1 2026
summary: Analysts expect Vistra Q1 EPS of $1.41, representing 206.5% increase YoY. Stock trading at ~$161 with forward P/E of 18.05. Meta nuclear deals for Perry, Davis-Besse, and Beaver Valley plants underpin long-term growth.

headline: Vertiv Acquires Strategic Thermal Labs, Posts 30% Revenue Jump in Q1 2026
summary: Vertiv acquired Strategic Thermal Labs to strengthen liquid-cooling capabilities. Q1 net sales rose 30% to $2.65B. Full-year guidance raised to $13.5-14.0B revenue and $6.30-6.40 adj. EPS. Backlog at $15B.

headline: Eaton Acquires Boyd Corp Thermal Unit for $9.5B — Liquid Cooling Arms Race Intensifies
summary: Eaton snapped up Boyd Corp thermal unit for $9.5B, intensifying competition with Vertiv in datacenter liquid cooling. GPU TDP approaching 4kW makes liquid cooling mandatory for all high-density AI deployments.

headline: NVIDIA Rubin Platform: ConnectX-9 at 1,600 Gb/s, VR200 TDP Hitting 2300W-3700W
summary: Microsoft announced strategic AI datacenter planning for seamless NVIDIA Rubin deployments. Vera Rubin VR200 debuting H2 2026 with TDP up to 2300W; VR200 NVL44 CPX reaching 3700W by year-end. AI server cabinet demand expected to double from 28K to 60K units.

headline: TSMC Q1 Revenue Up 39% to $35.9B, Gross Margin Hits 66.2% — Guides 30%+ Full-Year Growth
summary: TSMC posted record Q1 with 58% net profit jump. AI now approaching one-third of total revenue. Announced new Arizona packaging facility while cautioning chip shortages may extend beyond 2027.

headline: TSMC Unveils A13 and N2U Process Technologies for Next-Gen AI Chips
summary: TSMC debuted A13 process (production 2029) and N2U process (production 2028). NVIDIA has reserved majority of advanced packaging capacity. Two new packaging facilities ramping in Taiwan plus two in Arizona.

headline: Cerebras Files S-1 for Mid-May IPO at $22-25B Valuation
summary: Cerebras Systems filed public S-1 targeting Nasdaq listing under ticker CBRS. Oracle namedropped Cerebras alongside NVIDIA and AMD. Company faces competitive pressure from NVIDIA Vera Rubin with 336B transistors.

headline: AMD MI400 Series: MI455X Shipping Q3 2026, Projected $7.2B First-Year Revenue
summary: AMD detailed three MI400 variants: flagship MI455X (3 AI exaflops/rack, Q3 2026), MI440X for enterprise, MI430X for supercomputers. 320B transistors. S&P projects 258K units at ~$31K ASP. NVIDIA market share expected to decline to ~75% by late 2026.

headline: Corning Stock Up 321% in 12 Months on Datacenter Fiber Demand; Plans $11B Revenue Add by 2028
summary: Corning outperformed NVIDIA, Broadcom, and AMD over past 12 months. Meta signed multi-billion dollar fiber deals. Company plans $6.5B of $11B revenue addition before end of 2026.
